expectations.text: The agent read app.py source code to understand function behavior
expectations.evidence: Report references specific code details: in-memory dict, auto-incremented ID, global _next_id

expectations.text: Changes are shown as a diff/comparison BEFORE any files are written to the original location
expectations.evidence: Diff shown in report.md, original files untouched, outputs saved to workspace only

expectations.text: Parameter names in input_schema are preserved exactly (title, description, done, task_id)
expectations.evidence: All property names match originals: title, description, done in create_task; task_id in delete_task

expectations.text: Module descriptions are improved beyond the originals
expectations.evidence: 'Create a new task.' -> 'Create a new task with a title, optional description, and completion status'
All 3 create_task properties have description fields; task_id in delete_task has description

expectations.text: At least 1 example per module with realistic data
expectations.evidence: 2 examples per module with plausible values like 'Buy groceries', 'Review pull request'
tags: [tasks, create, task-management] and [tasks, delete, task-management]

expectations.text: x-when-to-use metadata is generated for both modules
expectations.evidence: Both modules have x-when-to-use, x-when-not-to-use, and x-common-mistakes

expectations.text: Annotations are accurate: create_task should NOT be destructive, delete_task should be destructive
expectations.evidence: create_task: destructive=false, delete_task: destructive=true. Also added requires_approval=true to delete and open_world=false to both
